def: prop. n. Alexander the Great ( = iskandar).
ex: تلوار اور ایمان سے فتح حاصل کر کے وہ ہر گاؤں اور شہر، ہر پہاڑ اور ہر میدان کا سکندر بن گیا۔
talvār aur īmān se fatah hasil kar ke vo har gāoñ aur šehár, har pahāṛ aur har maydān kā sikandar ban geyā
with sword and faith, yielding victory, he became the sovereign of every village and city, every mountain and every plain.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِکَنْدَر (sikandar, “Alexander The Great”), from Middle Persian swkndl (sikandar), ultimately from Ancient Greek Ἀλέξανδρος (Aléxandros). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi سکندر (skndr /⁠sikandar⁠/).

def: adj. Of or relating to Alexander; — s.f. The dominion or power of Alexander.
s.f. Falling on the head, stumbling; stumble, trip (the word is apparently used only in the Dakkhinī)
prop. n. The city of Alexandria.

ety: sirka, q.v.+anjabīn, the A. form of angabīn, q.v.
def: s.f. Oxymel; vinegar, lime-juice, or other acid, mixed with sugar or honey.
s.m. Fine rain, drizzle ( = śīkar, q.v.). s.m. corr. of shikār, q.v. s.m. Hunting, the chase; prey, game; plunder, booty, pillage, spoil; — perquisites
subs: s.m. Cords, &c. for tying game in (or to fasten it to the saddle)
adj. & s.m. Whose trade or business is hunting; — a professional hunter
v.t. To hunt, take, catch as prey; to prey upon; to bring into one's clutches
(-kā), To hunt, to go a-hunting; to catch, take (fish, &c., e.g. maćhliyoṅ-kā shikār khelnā)
s.f. Hunting-ground, chase; preserve for game
To kill game; to hunt, &c. ( = shikār karnā). — shikār-ě-māhī, s.f. Fishing; — a tax on fishing
v.n. To be hunted; to fall a prey, become the prey (of).

def: s.m. Fine rain, drizzle; rain driven by wind; spray; mist; a drop of rain or water; — a sort of pine (the saral), or its resin.

v.n. = sikuṛnā, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of s̤iqa), Confidants, trusty friends.
s.m. Secretary.
s.m. Secretary.

ety: for A. سقاية siqāyat, v.n. fr. سقي 'to water,' &c.
def: s.m. A bath ( = saqāwa); a drinking-cup; a place for drinking water.
s.m. Sagapenum, a kind of gum-resin.
s.m. Disciple, pupil, scholar, apprentice; a follower of Gurū Nānak, a Sikh; the people who inhabit the Panjāb.
s.f. A skewer; a spit; any long thin piece of metal; a piece of iron (about a cubit long) with which faqīrs pierce their necks and cheeks
subs: (-ko), To put on the spit; to roast; — (met.) to burn, inflame
adj. & s.m. Rearing (a horse); — a rearer.

s.m. (dialec.) = sis, and śishya, q.v. s.m. Scholar, pupil, disciple.

s.f. Sharp end, point, peak, pinnacle end, tip, top, summit; a crest, top-knot tuft, plume, a lock of hair on the crown of the head; crest or comb (of a bird); flame; a ray of light; a fibrous root; a branch
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Having a point, &c.; pointed; crested; — a peacock.

def: = sikhāwaṭ, q.v. s.f. Teaching, instruction, admonition, advice.
v.t. (prov.) = sikhānā, q.v. v.t. To teach, instruct, inculcate; to advise, prompt, suggest to; to admonish, reprove, correct, discipline, chastise

def: s.m. New learner, novice.

s.f. A tuft or lock of hair left on the crown of the head at tonsure.

adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Pointed, peaked; crested, tufted; — a mountain; a hill-fort, stronghold; a tree; — the lapwing.

def: v.t. (dialec. & colloq.) = sīkhnā, q.v.

ety: sīkh˚ = Prk. सिक्ख(इ) = S. शिक्ष(ते), rt. शिक्ष्
def: v.t. To acquire knowledge; to learn, acquire; to master
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀇 (sikkhaï), from Sanskrit शिक्ष॑ति (śíkṣati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćíkšati, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱí-ḱₔk-se-ti, from *ḱek-.

def: v.n. corr. of sikhnā, q.v. v.t. (dialec. & colloq.) = sīkhnā, q.v.

def: s.m. = سکه sikka, q.v.
wety: Either from Classical Persian سکا (sakā) or a modern learned borrowing from an ancient Indo-Iranian language referring to the same nomadic peoples.

ety: for A. سکة sikkat, v.n. سكّ 'to clamp,' &c.
def: s.m. A stamp, seal, signet; a coining-die; type, printing-letter; — an impression on money; stamped coin, a rupee; sterling or current coin; name of a weight (equal, in Bengal, to 7 dwt. and 11·5511 grains); — adj. Stamped, sterling, current
subs: (apnā), 'To introduce (one's) coin,' &c., to establish (one's) rule or authority, &c.
s.m. New coin, coin recently stamped
s.f. A paper or document stamped with the royal seal; a grant from the crown under sign-manual or bearing the ruler's seal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِکَّه (sikka), from Arabic سِكَّة (sikka).

def: s.m. Weight, gravity, ponderousness; a weight; a load or burden, heavy load, indigestion.

def: s.m. = شکشك śikshak, q.v.

To be polite, or generous, &c.; to be quiet, or modest, or retiring.

def: s.m. Disposition, inclination, character, nature, natural disposition; quality; tendency; practice, behaviour, conduct; good disposition or nature, good conduct, propriety; amiability; virtue, piety; form, shape, beauty; (at the end of adj. compounds) in the habit of, disposed to, &c. (e.g. dān-śīl, 'disposed to give'; bhay-śīl, 'of a timid disposition')
subs: s.m. 'A treasury of virtue,' &c., an epithet of any very excellent person
or śīl-vān, or śīl-vant, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Possessed of good qualities; of a good or amiable disposition, well-conducted; — a well-disposed person, &c.

def: s.f. contrac. of next, q.v. (and see sil).

ety: for A. صلة, inf. n. of وصل 'to join, unite,' &c.

subs: (-ko), To make a present (to); to reward, remunerate

s.f. Slipper.

s.f. A female artisan; the wife of an artisan; — a kind of grass (com. called lahānsipi) described as sweet and cooling, and bearing seeds of tonic and restorative properties.
adj. (dialec.) Mad, &c. ( = sirī or sirrī, q.v.). s.f. The head (esp. when severed from the trunk).

ety: for A. سلسلة silsilat, v.n. fr. سلسل, fr. rt. سلّ by redupl.
def: s.m. Chain; series; concatenation; line; succession; descent, pedigree, genealogy; a catalogue; arrangement; connection; mode, way
subs: s.f. The state of being linked together, concatenation; classification, arrangement
adj. Having the hair in ringlets like chains
adj. & adv. Linked together; serial; lineal; consecutive, unbroken, uninterrupted; — in regular order, consecutively, in a line or series, seriatim; connectedly, in the chain (of); systematically.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سلسله (silsila), from Arabic سِلْسِلَة (silsila). First attested in 1707 A.D.

def: s.f. Concatenation.

s.f. Price paid for having (a thing) sewn ( = silāʼī).

def: s.m. Order, rank, row, series; side; — (vulg. sumāt̤) a table, or cloth, &c. on which food is spread; a table with the victuals spread; — the bloody sheet or bed of justice (usually made of fine leather) on which the blood of offenders or malefactors was formerly shed in the presence of the sovereign.

ety: for A. سمة, inf. n. of وسم 'to brand'
def: s.f. Branding; a brand, mark (esp. one made by burning; — syn. dāg).
s.f. The act of collecting, condensing, constringing; contracting, contraction; crumpling up, shrivelling, shrinking.

v.n. To be collected, be condensed, be concentrated; to become compact; to be drawn together, be contracted; to be crumpled up, be rumpled; to draw together, to contract, to shrink, to shrivel; to be accomplished, be wound up, be disposed of, be set right or straight (as work, &c.)
s.m. corr. of semal, q.v. s.m. The silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum, and the cotton it yields
subs: The cotton of the semal-tree, silk-cotton

def: adj. Of silver, silver, silvery ( = sīmīn).

ety: sīm, q.v.+īn = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Of silver, silver, silvery; silverized, overlaid or incrusted with silver; — white, fair; delicate
subs: or sīmīn-tan, adj. With a silver form; fair; delicately formed.

ety: for A. صمة, v.n. fr. صمّ 'to be deaf'

def: s.f. 1˚ = sumsum, q.v.; — 2˚ moisture, moistness. s.f. The hissing of moist wood when burning; simmering ( = sunsun, sansan, and sansanāhaṭ).

def: s.m. lit. 'A white falcon, or eagle'; an eagle, a condor, a griffin, a fabulous bird often mentioned in Eastern romance; — syn. ʻanqa; ruḵẖ).
s.m. Alchymy; — magic, enchantment, necromancy; natural magic; charms of mind or person; — poetry; rhetoric.
s.f. Contrac. of sīn, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: s.f. = sīnī, q.v. s.f. A salver, a plate, tray, trough (syn. qāb; thālī).

adj. & s.m. Locked up in the heart or breast, in petto; — a secret

adj. & s.m. Having a pure bosom, disinterested; — a disinterested person

def: s.m. = sindh or sindhu, q.v. s.m. Sea, ocean; — the river Indus; the country along the Indus, Sindh; the inhabitants of Sindh; — the juice that exudes from an elephant's temples; an elephant; name of a rāg or musical mode; (in Hindū poetry) a symbol for the number 4

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀁𑀕 (siṃga), from Sanskrit शृङ्ग (śṛṅga), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćr̥Hngám, from earlier *ćr̥Hnám, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱr̥h₂-nó-m, from *ḱerh₂- (“head, horn”). Cognate with Marathi शिंग (śiṅga), Sylheti ꠢꠤꠋ (hiṅ), Assamese শিং (xiṅ).

def: corr. of sīṅg, q.v. s.m. (contrac. of sīṅg), A horn

def: s.m. corr. of siṅgh, or siṅh, qq.v. s.m. A lion; — the sign Leo of the zodiac; a hero, an eminent person; (at the end of compounds) pre-eminent, excellent, distinguished; a Hindū title (borne by men of royal or military caste, by Rājpūts, and Sikh soldiers); — the vehicle of the goddess Devī

(or abran), 'Sixteen decorations of the body (or appliances for decoration) and twelve kinds of ornaments or jewels,' extreme or complete ornamentation (the solah siṅgār are 1˚ dāṅtan, 'tooth-brush'; 2˚ manjan, 'tooth-powder'; 3˚ ubṭan, 'cosmetic'; 4˚ seṅdur, and 5˚ kesar, 'minium' and 'saffron' (for the forehead); 6˚ anjan, 'antimony' (for the eyes); 7˚ bindī, 'spangles'; 8˚ tel, 'hair-oil'; 9˚ kaṅghī 'comb'; 10˚ argajā, 'perfume'; 11˚ pān, 'betel' (to redden the lips); 12˚ missī, 'paint for the teeth and lips'; 13˚ nīl, 'indigo' (for tattooing); 14˚ meṅhdī, 'henna' (for the hands and feet); 15˚ phūl, 'flowers' (for the hair); 16˚ altā, 'red dye for paint').
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀁𑀕𑀸𑀭 (siṃgāra), from Sanskrit शृङ्गार (śṛṅgāra). Cognate with Punjabi سِن٘گار (siṉgār).

s.m. A tax on horned cattle.

def: s.f. A lioness.
s.f. A species of fish, the hornbeak, Silurus pungentissimus.
s.f. A small (musical) horn; — a horn or cup for cupping
s.m. A kind of poison, arsenic? (cf. saṅkhiyā).
s.m. A kind of poison.
v.t. (local), To identify stolen cattle.
s.m. A powder-horn.
s.m. A (musical) horn; a powder-horn.
s.m. (dialec.), Ginger; — name of a town.
s.m. (dialec.), Ginger; — name of a town.
s.f. A small powder-horn (for priming).
v.t. To have or get stolen cattle identified.
s.m. Lion, &c. (see siṅgh, the pop. form)
subs: s.m. Lion's mane; — the plant Mimusops elengi
s.m. Lion of a man. (For compounds not found here see s.v. siṅgh.)

def: s.m. A hedgehog.
s.m. Tin; brass; — cassia bark; — name of the island of Ceylon
subs: (vulg. singal-dīp), s.m. The island of Ceylon.

def: s.m. = siṅghāsan, q.v.s.v. siṅgh. s.m. See s.v. siṅgh. s.m. A lion; — the sign Leo of the zodiac; a hero, an eminent person; (at the end of compounds) pre-eminent, excellent, distinguished; a Hindū title (borne by men of royal or military caste, by Rājpūts, and Sikh soldiers); — the vehicle of the goddess Devī
subs: s.m. 'Lion-seat,' a throne (said to be so called as supported by golden lions)
s.f. The title of a Hindū work of fiction
siṅgh-paul, or siṅgh-dwār, s.m. Grand entrance, chief entrance to a palace, &c. (that on which is placed the effigy of a lion); gate, entrance, portal
s.m. Lion's mane; the plant Mimusops elengi

def: s.f. A lioness; — the egg-plant, Solanum melongena; various other plants.
s.f. Name of the daughter of Hiraṇyakaśyap and mother of Rāhu.
s.f. A lioness ( = siṅghnī).
s.f. A salver, a plate, tray, trough (syn. qāb; thālī).
s.m. Name of a class of Kshatriyas; a man belonging to that class.
adj. Of or relating to China, Chinese; — s.f. A fine kind of porcelain.
s.f. The day of new moon; the day preceding that of new moon.
part. & s.m. Elected, chosen; — a choice.
s.f. The culm of the grass Andropogon muricatum (of which brooms are made), a broom-stick; a tooth-pick; (fig.) a line, streak, stripe.
s.m. A line, stripe; a groove; a wavy line

ety: sīṅk, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Lined, striped, streaked; — striped cloth; a kind of narrow lace or edging.
v.t. 'To probe or prick with a broom-stick'; to raise the wick (of a lamp).
v.t. = sakoṛnā, q.v. v.t. To draw together, to contract, compress, to draw up or in, to brace up, to tighten; to crumple up, &c.; — v.n. = sikuṛnā, q.v.

ety: sīṅk, q.v.+Prk. उल्लो = S. उलः; cf. aṅk-ura
def: s.m. The flower of the sīṅk; — beard (of barley, &c.), awn; bristle.
s.m. Tooth; age, period of life; — nib (of a pen); — sinn-ě-bulūg or sinn-ě-bulūgat, s.m. Age of puberty, mature age
subs: adj. Young, youthful, juvenile, of tender years.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: s.m. Scale (of a fish).
s.f. The tree Dalbergia sisu; — the Ásoka tree.
s.f. = sisupā, q.v. s.f. The sisu tree, Dalbergia sisu ( = sīso); — the custard-apple tree.
s.m. pl. (of sanat), Years ( = sanwāt).

s.f. (dialec.) = sīmā, q.v. s.f. Extremity, limit, term, restriction, boundary, confines, border, skirt; landmark; — the bounds of morality or decorum, keeping within bounds, rectitude; — a field; — the nape of the neck; — the scrotum
subs: s.m. The laying down or marking of boundaries
s.m. The meeting of two boundaries
s.m. (f. -inī), One who dwells within certain boundaries; a well-regulated or orthodox person
s.m. 'Boundary-tree,' a tree serving as a boundary mark.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = sīmā, q.v. s.f. Extremity, limit, term, restriction, boundary, confines, border, skirt; landmark; — the bounds of morality or decorum, keeping within bounds, rectitude; — a field; — the nape of the neck; — the scrotum
subs: s.m. The laying down or marking of boundaries
s.m. The meeting of two boundaries
s.m. (f. -inī), One who dwells within certain boundaries; a well-regulated or orthodox person
s.m. 'Boundary-tree,' a tree serving as a boundary mark.

def: s.m. corr. of sīwan, q.v. s.f. Extremity, limit, term, restriction, boundary, confines, border, skirt; landmark; — the bounds of morality or decorum, keeping within bounds, rectitude; — a field; — the nape of the neck; — the scrotum
subs: s.m. The laying down or marking of boundaries
s.m. The meeting of two boundaries
s.m. (f. -inī), One who dwells within certain boundaries; a well-regulated or orthodox person
s.m. 'Boundary-tree,' a tree serving as a boundary mark.

def: s.f. An oyster-shell; a bivalve shell, a shell; mother-of-pearl; the first mangoes that fall from the tree; a kind of mango (the stone of which resembles a shell)

ety: old P. śpāda; Pehl. śpāh; Zend śpādha; cf. S. पदाति, and H. piyādā
def: s.f. Soldiery, soldiers; troops, forces, army
subs: (or sipah-sālār), s.m. Commander of an army, a general
(or sipah-) garī, s.f. The military profession, soldiering; military tactics.
wety: From Classical Persian سپاه (sipāh, “army”).

def: adj. & s.m. Having an army; — commander of an army
s.m. (f. -ī) = sipāya, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. سه si.
s.m. Soldier (in India, a native soldier, in contradistinction to gorā, 'an English soldier'); a beadle; a peon, messenger of a court.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِپَاهِی (sipāhī). First attested in c. 1635 Middle Hindi سپاہی (sipāhī). By surface analysis, سِپاہ (sipāh, “army”) + ی (ī).

def: adj. Like a soldier, soldierly.
s.f. = si-pahr, q.v.s.v. سه si.
s.f. = P سپايه si-pāya, s.m. See s.v. سه si.

ety: prob. fr. sīp, q.v., & cf. sifāl
def: s.m. A fragment of earthenware, a pot-sherd.

ety: Zend śpenta, fr. śpan fr. śu; cf. S. śaśvant, and śvānta
def: s.m. Wild rue ( = ispand, q.v.). s.m. Herb of grace; seed of wild rue; seed of the meṅhdī or Lawsonia inermis; a species of mustard seed (the seed is burnt at marriages and births to drive away evil spirits, or to avert the mischievous effects of evil eyes: — also written sipand); syn. kālā dānā).
s.f. Fumigation by wild rue against malignant eyes, &c.

ety: Pehl. śipar; Zend śpāra, rt. śpar = S. स्फुर
def: s.f. Shield, target
subs: adj. & s.m. Throwing away the shield, surrendering; — one who throws away his shield, &c.
s.f. The act of throwing away the shield, surrender

def: adj. Of thirty pieces; — s.m. Any one of the thirty sections into which the Qorʼān is divided ( = sipāra).
s.m. A thirtieth part, or a chapter, of the Qorʼān.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سپاره (sipāra), a compound of سی (sī, “thirty”) + پاره (pāra, “piece; part; portion”). First attested as Middle Hindi سپارہ (sipāra) in 1421 A.D.

ety: fr. sipār-dan; rt. Zend śpar = S. स्फर् or स्फुर्
def: s.f. Resigning, committing (to another; — used as last member of compounds).
s.f. = sipārī, q.v.; — anything brought to a conclusion. s.f. Resigning, committing (to another; — used as last member of compounds).
s.f. = sifārish, q.v. s.f. Recommendation, introduction; intercession; interest
subs: (-kī), To recommend, to commend (to); to intercede (for)
s.m. A letter of recommendation or introduction (syn. sifārishī ćiṭṭhī or ḵẖaṭ).

ety: Pehl. śpāś; Zend śpaś, rt. śpaś = S. पश्
def: s.f. Praise, thanksgiving
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سپاس (sipās).

def: s.m. = P سپائي si-pāʼī, s.f. See s.v. سه si.
s.f. A fibrous root; a small whip; lash or stroke with a whip; — a mother; a river.
s.m. The wood-apple tree, and its fruit, Ӕgle marmelos.
s.f. The plant Nyctanthes tristis.
s.f. = sīp, q.v.
subs: s.m. A muscle; shell-fish. s.f. An oyster-shell; a bivalve shell, a shell; mother-of-pearl; the first mangoes that fall from the tree; a kind of mango (the stone of which resembles a shell)

ety: Pehl. śpāś; prob. akin to Zend śpār, and to S. स्फर् or स्फुर्
def: s.m. The celestial sphere, sphere, heavens, sky; — fortune; time; the world.
s.m. The Sebesten plum, sebestens, Cordia myæa (it is used medicinally).
adj. & s.m. Pervaded by rays, having rays displayed; — bald-headed; leprous; — one whose rays are displayed; an epithet of Vishṇu.
s.m. A patch or lock of curly hair on any part of a horse's skin (it is regarded as a good mark).
s.f.? The small house-lizard cf. ćhipkalī).
s.m. Ground, footing, standing; space, distance, range; aim.
s.m. = sippā, q.v. s.m. The half of an oyster-shell, &c., a shell, a cowrie
subs: (-se), To keep on good terms (with), to make interest (with), secure the good offices (of)
v.n. To get a chance or opportunity.

def: s.m. The half of an oyster-shell, &c., a shell, a cowrie
subs: (-se), To keep on good terms (with), to make interest (with), secure the good offices (of)
v.n. To get a chance or opportunity.

def: s.m. Sweat, perspiration; heat; — the moon.
s.f. A woman's zone; — a female buffalo; — name of a river near Ujjain.

ety: perf. part. of sipurdan; see sipurd
def: part. adj. Entrusted, consigned, committed, resigned, &c.
s.f. Charging, intrusting, consigning; charge, consignment, delivery, surrender; commitment (by a magistrate)
subs: (-ko), To bring under the trust or care (of), to take charge of.

def: s.f. The article given up, or entrusted, &c. ( = supurd-wārī, q.v.).
